title: 2023年12月大学英语六级真题(第1套)

sections.title: Part I Writing
sections.directions: For this part, you are allowed 30 minutes to write an essay that begins with the sentence "As is known to all, gaining a sound knowledge of the basics is of vital importance for students to master an academie subject.”You can make comments, cite examples or use your personal experiences to develop your essay. You should write at least L50 words but no more than 200 words (not including the sentence given).
sections.prompt: As is known to all, gaining a sound knowledge of the basics is of vital importance for students to master an academie subject.

options.A: It is clear that he is expected to enjoy a healthy life.
options.B: There is nothing wrong with his digestive system.
options.C: There is some indication of an issue with his blood circulation.
options.D: He doesn't know he has long been suffering from poor health.

questions.explanation: 到什么？ A）很明显，他被期望享受健康的生活。 C）有迹象表明他的血液循环有问题。 B）他的消化系统没有问题。 D）他不知道他的健康状况长期以来一直不好。 解+对话并买交代亍勇王的誉者绪巢:法圣好勇王说:…•“莪逐看你的福音结巢「是不算酒意而1结巢段看商 析:题。没有任何消化问题的迹象。”由此可知，B项与对话内容相符。 2 六级2023.12 ］ 第一装

options.A: Mistaking symptoms of illness for stress.
options.B: Complaining they are being overworked.
options.C: Being unaware of the stress they are under.
options.D: Suffering from illness without recognising it.

questions.explanation: 尸纤话市面:戛壬说勇王感蔻示舒胭时能莫茵为・汪手蒙重前王祚莉区万；・勇王・ 却反鼓说百巨以•段感到汪庆 析 力，女士说男士描述的这种看法是普遍的，随后指明这一普遍看法是指“许多承受压力的人没有意识到这 一点”，故C项与对话内容相符。

options.A: Prescribe some medication for him.
options.B: Give him another physical check-up.
options.C: Explain to him the common consequence of stress.
options.D: Buy some sleeping pills for him from the drugstore.

questions.explanation: 释压力的常见后果。 D）从药店给他买些安眠药。 解:留话市面:•・勇王福而五王百巨施演海福巢施工诲不殍宛显煮/面事:玉王籥解说仓国瓶兼需戛扇另希莱 析 的常见后果，并说：“我将给你开一些专门的安眠药。”由此可知，A项与对话内容相符。

options.A: It calls for responsible management.
options.B: It proves to be quite profitable.
options.C: It is remarkably promising.
options.D: It is full of competition.

questions.explanation: +好法羸后；…王王谨及勇王信功不宜话:…笄面荷勇王看在石苏藩时以藏不秘而王柞墓「勇王而容振…曦• 析 是一名餐厅经理，这个行业竞争非常激烈。”由此可知，男士所从事的行业充满了竞争，D项与对话内容 相符。 Conversation Two 。 ・听力原文• W: Today on People in the News, our guest is John Williams. The name may not sound familiar to you, but John was once an acclaimed basketball player. John, you stunned fans by leaving the sport at just 25. Why did you retire so early? M: Meg, I loved being an athlete, but I didn't love being a celebrity. I was in the limelight when I was still a high school student and went professional right after high school graduation, which was a mistake. [5] I was a shy kid and I wasn't ready fbr all the media attention. W: But walking away from millions of dollars at the height of your career, most people wouldn't be able to resist the lure of such a high salary. When you left the sport, there was speculation that you were having issues with your teammates or even an injur

options.A: To avoid being in the limelight.
options.B: To seek medical help for his injury.
options.C: To pursue a less competitive career.
options.D: To stay away from his hostile teammates.

questions.explanation: 工 析 对话开头，女士问男士为什么这么早退役，男士回答说自己不喜欢当名人，并说道：“我是一个害羞的孩 子，而且我还没有准备好接受所有媒体的关注。”由此可知，约翰年纪轻轻就从篮球运动退役，是为了避 免成为焦点，A项与对话内容相符。

options.A: It has ups and downs.
options.B: It proves rewarding.
options.C: It does not last long.
options.D: It is not so profitable.

questions.explanation: 了 析 萧南.市面;..勇壬说；莪师道好天.数球瓦莱篦…（打）.•窿森并示息二顼会及南联通:…诉以莪植.茬冠军转 的时候改变职业。”由此可知，C项与对话内容相符。

options.A: He was a financial advisor.
options.B: He suffered from poor health.
options.C: He became a basketball coach.
options.D: He was back in the news.

questions.explanation: 到20年后的约翰•威廉姆斯的什么情况？ A）他是一名财务顾问。 C）他成为了一名篮球教练。 B）他健康状况不好。 D）他又上了新闻。 解. 析 对话中女士对男士说：“好吧，那是20年前的事了，现在你又上新闻了。”由此可知，D项与对话内容相 符。A项是根据男士所说的financial advisers设置的干扰项，男士原话是他有很棒的财务顾问，而不是他自 己成为了财务顾问，故排除。

options.A: Study issues of public health.
options.B: Alleviate the obesity problem.
options.C: Raise sufficient public funding.
options.D: Train young basketball players.

questions.explanation: 肥胖问题。 D）训练年轻的篮球运动员。 解!需洁盛后;…勇王神百巨天孽前专应戛公兵王至「植亍茄蓟葩涯黯流行而拜童桂:霍日王前20车童：…二•看就 析:致力于缓解这一问题。由此可知，B项与对话内容相符。. Section 鱼 Passage One 。 ・听力原文• Kate Atkinson was bom in York, England, in 1951. She worked hard to gain her credentials as an author. She studied English Literature at University in Scotland. After graduating in 1974, she researched a doctorate on American Literature. Later, she taught at the university she graduated from, and began writing short stories in 1981. [9] She began writing fbr women's magazines after winning the 1986 Woman's Own Short Story Competition. Her first novel, Behind the Scenes at the Museum, won the 1995 UK Book of the Year award. The book is set in Yorkshire and has been adapted fbr radio, theatre, and TV. She has written two plays fbr a theatre in Edinburgh—the first was called Nice and the second Abandonment, performed as part of the Edinburgh Festival in August

options.A: When she started teaching at Edinburgh University in Scotland.
options.B: While she was doing her doctoral studies on American Literature.
options.C: After publishing her first novel Behind the Scenes at the Museum.
options.D: After winning the 1986 Woman's Own Short Story Competition.

questions.explanation: ；受簟并买棘芬盘菰…夜6•军;…施茬•”安E百巨葡适篇示说E匕静；…市叙奚：三后并诏身妥佳莱患驾天簟; 析. 由此可知，凯特•阿特金森是在作品获奖之后开始为女性杂志撰稿的，故选项D正确。文中并未提到她 所任教的大学的名字，故选项A排除；文中虽然提到她1974年毕业后攻读了美国文学博士学位，以及后 来出版第一部小说《博物馆的幕后故事》，但前者是她在为女性杂志撰稿之前，后者是她在为女性杂志 撰稿之后，故选项B 、C都排除。

options.A: The themes of love and loss.
options.B: The code of human behaviour.
options.C: The sins and flaws of eccentrics.
options.D: The manners of fashionable circles.

options.A: They are usually ignorant of complex human relations.
options.B: They successfully imitate the manners of celebrities.
options.C: They often get rewarded instead of being punished.
options.D: They are generally looked down upon in society.

options.A: It is what members use to alleviate tension in a team.
options.B: It is what employers are increasingly seeking after.
options.C: It is conducive to getting over a recession.

questions.explanation: 团队关系紧张的方法。 C）它有助于摆脱经济衰退。 B）这是雇主们越来越追求的。 D）这是学习一项新任务的必要条件。 解，％簟笄英曲盘由荷面；…%秆么说盅应用为莫茬加场币萋镶藤面二顼童萋及篦?. ・接卡莱第三有薪面容亍透 析|个问题：适应能力是雇主越来越追求的一项技能。因此，选项B正确，选项中的seeking after是文中looking ] for的同义转述。

options.B: Follow innovative ideas.
options.C: Achieve recognition duly.
options.D: Accumulate work experience.

options.B: Workers use brains more than muscles.
options.C: People usually work flexible hours.
options.D: People often work in teams.

options.B: Anticipate setbacks well in advance.
options.C: Decide on new priorities speedily.

questions.explanation: 家B受）簟 很羸 好后地.提 福前 且预 •…贡 料•挫洁折谣。 泳壬篦露菱花■星而远D蕾）甯自矗信衽地足坚茨持庠最:… 初舁的.茬 目藁 标国 。事禧行不造舟短由版芥前商毒. 析： \议。因此选项C正确，选项中的Decide on new priorities和speedily分别对应原文中的reprioritize和quickly。 Section 维 Recording One 。 ・听力原文• [16] What makes humans different fh）m other species? Some philosophers argue ifs morals or ethics, while some scientists assert it's our greater cognitive development. But I argue that the main difference is our desire to combat routine. This makes being creative a biological mandate, as what we seek in art and technology is surprise, not simply a fulfillment of expectations. As a result, a wild imagination has characterized the history of our species. We build intricate habitats, devise complex recipes fbr our food, wear clothes that reflect constant changes in fashion, communicate with elaborate signs, symbols, and sounds, and travel between habitats on wings and wheels of our own desig

options.A: What differentiates people from animals.
options.B: Why philosophers disagree with scientists.
options.C: Where humans' great cognitive capacity originates.
options.D: When being creative becomes a biological mandate.

questions.explanation: ；. 析：在讲座开头，讲话者便提出了 “是什么让人类与其他物种不同”这个问题。由此很容易得出正确答案为 选项A。

options.A: It is what tells apart two adjacent generations.
options.B: It is what sharpens our appetite for novelty.
options.C: It is something only geniuses can achieve.
options.D: It is something every human being can do.

questions.explanation: ，. 析：讲话者在中间部分提到，许多人都认为只有天才才能创新，但讲话者相信，创新不是只有少数人才能做到 的事情。由此可知，选项D为正确答案。

options.A: It seeks inspiration for novel inventions.
options.B: It constantly absorbs new information.
options.C: It uses existing ideas to create new ones.
options.D: It repeats precedent on a regular basis.

questions.explanation: ；. 析： 讲话者提到，人类的大脑是依托先例运作的。我们把继承下来的想法组合在一起，形成新的形态。由此可 知，选项C为正确答案，同时可直接排除选项D。选项A中的inspiration和inventions讲座中虽然也有提到， 但这不是本题所提问的内容，故选项A排除。讲座中虽有提及a b so rb ,但描述的是创造者吸收过去的经 验，不符合题意，故选项B排除。 Recording Two @ • 听力原文• [19] Many dog owners would tell you that their dog somehow knows when they're ill or upset, and according to researchers who study dog cognition, those pet owneis are light. Dogs do know when their human companions are having a rough time. Not only can your dog sense when you have a cold, but domestic dogs have shown an aptitude for detecting both much smaller mood fluctuations and far more serious physical conditions. This is because dogs are extremely sensitive to changes in the people they're familiar with, and illness causes change. [20] If a person is infbcted with a virus or bacteria, for example, their odor will be abnormal, and dogs are able to smell that change even if the hum

options.A: Dogs know when their owners are not feeling well.
options.B: Dogs have the cognition for telling right from wrong.
options.C: Dogs have an aptitude for developing skills to interact with humans.
options.D: Dogs know when their human companions can no longer stand them.

questions.explanation: ； 析； B）狗有分辨对错的认知。 D）当狗的人类同伴再也不能忍受它们时，狗会知道。 讲话者在开头就提到，许多养狗人士会告诉你，当他们生病或心烦意乱时，他们的狗不知怎么就会知道，而 且根据研究狗认知的研究人员的说法，这些宠物主人的感觉是对的。由此很容易得出，选项A为正确答案。

options.A: They can readily detect different ill smells of viruses.
options.B: They can easily tell what bacteria cause odor change.
options.C: They are particularly sensitive to strange smells.
options.D: They have an extremely powerful sense of smell.

questions.explanation: ； 析： 讲话者在前半部分提到，如果一个人感染了病毒或细菌，他们的气味就会有异常，即使人类不能，狗也能 闻出这种气味的变化，因为狗的嗅觉比人类强大得多。由此可知，选项D为正确答案。

options.A: It can ensure owners suffer fewer chronic diseases.
options.B: It can benefit owners both physically and mentally.
options.C: It can reduce owners' risk of getting cancer or diabetes.
options.D: It can alert owners to the seriousness of their conditions.

questions.explanation: 工 析： 讲话者在最后提到，目前，有研究表明，养狗本身就有很多好处，可以促进健康和改善情绪。由此可知， 选项B为正确答案，“身心受益”是对原文bolster health and boost mood的概括。选项A是强干扰项，其中的 chronic diseases讲座中虽然也有提到，但讲的是对那些患有慢性疾病的人来说，狗是一种特别的安慰，而 非能确保主人少患慢性病，故排除。 Recording Three 。 ・听力原文♦ Earlier this month, the think-tank called Onward published a report, UA Question of Degree”, which argues that degrees in the creative arts are not good value fbr money. [22] Ministers, according to Onward, should “crack down on courses that offer extremely limited value for money to students ten years after graduation”, restricting the ability of such courses to recruit new students, if the average graduate earns below the student loans payment threshold. Courses like science, technology, engineering, and math, and economics, where the average graduate earns a lot, should be favored. The report provides insight into a government review which looks at how to reform technical e

options.A: Crack down on courses like science, technology, engineering and math.
options.B: Restrict the ability of creative arts courses to recruit new students.
options.C: Look at how to reform technical and vocational education.
options.D: Ensure creative arts students get better value for money.

questions.explanation: ； 析1 讲话者在开头提到，智库Onward发表了一份题为“学位问题”的报告，该报告认为创意艺术专业的学位 并非物有所值。Onward认为，部长们应该“严厉打击那些为毕业10年后的学生提供的性价比极其有限 的课程”，如果毕业生的平均收入低于学生贷款支付门槛，就应该限制这类课程招收新生的能力。由此 可知，Onward认为创意艺术专业给学生提供的课程性价比极其有限，应该限制创意艺术课程招收新生 的能力，故选项B为正确答案。讲话者明确提到，根据Onward的报告，科学、技术、工程、数学以及 经济学等毕业生平均收入较高的课程应受到青睐，故选项A错误。其余两个选项在讲话中并未提及，可 排除。

options.A: Seemingly reasonable.
options.B: Clearly well-grounded.
options.C: Extremely irrational.
options.D: Apparently simplistic.

questions.explanation: ；讲话者提到，乍一看，Onward的观点似乎有道理，故选项A为正确答案。其余三个选项在讲话中并未提 及，可排除。

options.A: A high proportion of them haven't tried to save money.
options.B: Most of them never hope to buy a house or to retire.
options.C: Forty percent of them earn less than E25,000 a year.
options.D: The majority of them have fairly well-paying jobs.

options.A: The context of a bank balance.
options.B: Britain's economy as a whole.
options.C: The specific degree a student earns.
options.D: Britain's defective educational system.

questions.explanation: S 讲话者在后半部分提到，问题并不真正在于那些攻读特定学位的学生，而在于整个经济。由此可知，选项 析 B 为正确答案，同时排除选项C 。其余两个选项在讲话中并未提及，可排除。 Part III (>) Reading Comprehension Section 画 。 •概览・ 本文节选自Discovery探索频道网站上2020年12月2 日发表的一篇标题为“Secret Tunnels Beneath the Desert，，的文章。本文主要讲述了莫哈韦沙漠的独特地理风貌和自然环境。在作者看来，它是户外爱好者 和摄影师的理想之地。 ◎ ・全文翻译• 沙漠具有欺骗性。乍一看，它看起来毫无生气、贫瘠且干燥至极。对于大多数去拉斯维加斯碰运气或者 前往大峡谷，在路上哼着歌穿过莫哈韦的路人来说，这只是一片［26］平坦之地，远处有一些山脉，更［27］容易 作为一部发生在火星上的电影的场景。然而，这片沙漠却［28］充满了生命、神秘和魔力。 莫哈韦沙漠每年的降雨量不足两英寸，和大多数沙漠一样，这里也是一片［29］极端之地。温度波动的范围 从严寒到［30］酷热不等，不仅在季节之间，甚至在同一天内也是如此。 莫哈韦国家保护区位于这片25,000平方英里广袤沙漠的中心。在那里管理和工作的人都戴着宽边帽，这 已经成为约塞米蒂这样的地方的［31］代名词。该保护区拥有丰富的历史、文化、生物多样性和无尽的机会来 ［32］激发你的兴趣，尤其是对于任何热爱户外活动的人来说。对于摄影师来说，要想驾驭这些未铺设的“道 路”网络，不仅需要耐心，还［33］可能需要一些备用轮胎，但回报是丰厚的。 在这里拍摄需要一双［34］结实的好鞋和大量的准备工作。要探索这个国家公园系统中的第三大公园，需要 大量的水、备用轮胎和满满一箱汽油。 然而，就像所有的沙漠一样，如果你有耐心，你会得到回报，因为它们往往会慢慢地揭示自己的秘密。 在我上一次旅行中，我惊讶地发现这一地区的很多地方都是由古火山和地质力量塑造而成的，其中大部分至今 仍保留着，这给该地区带来了一种［35］独特的感觉，也为拍摄绝佳照片提供了丰富多彩的背景。 。 ・答

sections.title: Section A · 选词填空
sections.passage:
Directions: In this section, there is a passage with ten blanks. You are required to select one word for each blank
from a list of choices given in a word bank following the passage. Read the passage through carefully before
making your choices. Each choice in the bank is identified by a letter. Please mark the corresponding letter for
each item on Answer Sheet 2 with a single line through the centre. You may not use any of the words in the bank
more than once.
The desert is deceiving. At first glance it looks lifeless, barren, and bone-dry. For most passersby humming
through the Mojave on their way to try their luck in Las Vegas or heading towards the Grand Canyon, it's
just a 26 stretch of land with some mountains in the distance and more.
27 to be a setting for a movie that
takes place on Mars. The desert, however, is. 28 with life, mystery, and magic.
The Mojave desert sees less than two inches of rain a year, and like most deserts, is a land of .

Temperature fluctuations vary from freezing to.

 hot, not only between seasons, but even within the same
day.
At the heart of this vast, 25,000-square-mile desert is Mojave National Preserve. The folks managing and
working there wear the wide-brimmed(宽边的)hats that have become.

_with places like Yosemite. The
preserve is rich with history, culture, biodiversity, and endless opportunities to. 32your interest,especially for
anyone who loves the outdoors. For a photographer, it requires patience and. 33 a few spare tires to maneuver
the network of unpaved “roads”, but the rewards are plenty.
Photographing here requires a good,. 34 pair of shoes and a lot of preparation.Plenty of water, spare tires,
and a full tank of gas are needed to explore this unit of the national park system, which is the third largest in the
country.

Like all deserts, though, if you are patient, you will be rewarded, as they often reveal their secrets slowly.
During my last outing, I was surprised to see how much of the area was shaped by ancient volcanoes and
geological forces, much of which remains today, giving the area a 35 feel and painting a colorful background
for great photographs

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text:
On the hills of central Kenya, almost lime-green with the sparkle of tea bushes in the sunlight, farmers
know all about climate change.“The rainy season is no longer predictable," says one."When it is supposed
to rain it doesn't, then it all comes at once." Climate change is an issue that will affect everyone on the
planet. For Africans its consequences will be particularly bitter: whereas other regions were able to grow
rich by burning coal and oil, Africa will pay much of the human price without having enjoyed the benefits.
“Africa only represents 2of global greenhouse-gas emissions but it is the continent that is expected to
suffer the most from climate impacts,” says Mafalda Duarte, who runs the World Bank's $8bn Climate
Investment Funds.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text:
Although there are huge uncertainties as to the precise impacts of climate change, enough is known to say that
global warming represents one of the main threats to Africa's prosperity. Parts of the continent are already
warming much more quickly than the average: temperatures in southern Africa have increased by about twice
the global rate over the past 50 years. Even if the world were to cut emissions enough to keep global warming
below 1.5℃, heat-waves would intensify in Africa and diseases would spread to areas not currently affected.
Farming would also be hit hard. About 40of the land now used to grow maize (玉米) would no longer be
suitable for it. Overall, it is estimated that maize yields would fall by 18-22%.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
Africa is particularly vulnerable, in part because it is already struggling to feed itself and it will have to vastly
increase yields and productivity if it is to put food on the plates of a fast-growing population, even without
climate change. The UN's Food and Agriculture Organisation reckons that by 2050 global food production
would have to rise by about 70over its level of 2009 to meet demand from a population that is growing in
numbers and appetite. Much of this new demand will be in Africa. Yet the continent already imports about
S50bn-worth of food a year and that figure is expected to more than double over the next five years. Self-
sufficiency is not Africa's goal, but the fact that it spends more money importing food than it does buying
capital goods suggests it has room for improvement.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text:
Finding out why is not hard. Most farms are tiny, ploughed by hand and reliant on rain. More than half of
Africa's people make their living from farming.Although its total harvest has climbed over the past few
decades, this is mainly because there are more people farming more land. But in many places there is no
spare land to farm. Plots in Rwanda are so small that you could fit 250 of them onto the average American
farm. And although output per worker has improved by more than half over the past 30 years in Africa, that
is still far behind the 2.5 times improvement in Asia. Yields of maize are generally less than two tonnes per
hectare, a fifth the level in America.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text:
The low productivity of African farmers is reflected in national economic statistics—despite absorbing
so much labour, farming generates just 15of GDP.“They can't even feed their families,” says Jennifer
Blanke, a vice-president of the African Development Bank in charge of agriculture.“Farm productivity hasn't
improved in many parts of Africa for 100 years."

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text:
One reason is that in the first few decades of independence, many African govermments neglected farming
as they focused on industrialising their economies. Others damaged it by pushing down the prices that state
monopolies paid for their crops in order to subsidise workers in cities with cheap food. Ghana taxed cocoa
(可可粉) exports so heavily that production collapsed by half between the 1960s and 1980s, despite a jump
in the global price of cocoa. Yet over the past two decades or so governments and donors have begun to look
again at farming as a way of providing jobs for the 13 million young people entering the workforce each year.
Much of the focus has been on getting small farmers to use fertiliser and, more important, better seeds. The
results can be impressive. Improved varieties of sorghum(高梁), for instance, can produce a crop that is 40%
larger than the usual variety. Infrastructure is important. A World Bank irigation project in Ethiopia helped
farmers increase their potato harvest from about 8 tonnes per hectare to 35 tonnes.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text:
Better techniques help, too. Small coffee farmers in Kenya are able to increase their incomes by 40y
following a few simple guidelines on caring for their bushes, such as trimming all but three of their stems.
Many of their neighbours do not follow the advice, because it seems counter-intuitive. More stems ought
to lead to more coffee beans, they say. Yet after seeing those following the advice get bigger harvests for a
season or two, many others start doing the same.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text:
One way of spreading knowledge is to link farms to big buyers of their harvests. When Diageo, a British
drinks giant, built a brewery in western Kenya, it wanted to use local crops to make a beer cheap enough to
compete with illicit home brew. It organised farmers into groups, improved supply chains for them to get
seeds and fertiliser and then agreed to buy their grain.It now provides a market to about 17,000 farmers.
Across the region it has doubled its use of local raw material to about 80over five years, says John O'Keeffe,
who runs its Africa business.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text:
An even more important change is the move from traditional farming to building businesses that can
profitably bring technology and investment to small farmers. Taita Ngetich, a young Kenyan, was studying
engineering when he wanted to earn a little money on the side. He scraped together 20,000 Kenyan shillings
(about $200) to plant tomatoes. Everything went wrong. The crop was attacked by pests."Then there was
a massive flood that swallowed all our capital," he says. Mr Ngetich persevered by looking into buying a
greenhouse to protect his plants from bugs and rain. The cheapest ones cost more than $2,500 each, so he
designed his own for half the price. Soon neighbouring farmers started placing orders with him, and now his
firm, llluminum Greenhouses, has sold more than 1,400 greenhouses that provide livelihoods to about 6,000
people. The business does not stop there; he also supplies fertiliser, high-quality seedlings and smart sensors
that increase yields

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text:
Illuminum's success shows how technology can help even small farms become more productive. Because
such a large share of Africa's population earns a living from agriculture,even small improvements in

productivity can lift the incomes of millions of people. But over the longer run small-scale farming can go
only so far, especially in the face of climate change and population pressure.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text:
"If we really want to lift people out of poverty we have to finance projects that will get them an income of at
least $100 a month so that they can pay for health care and education," says Mr Ngetich."Projects that give
them an extra $2 a month from growing beans or maize aren't going to get them there." Getting those big
jumps will need better jobs in factories and cities.

questions.stem: It is said that agricultural productivity in many African countries has remained low for a century.

Building connections between farms and major purchasers of their produce can promote African farmers' use
of advanced farming techniques.

Parts of Africa are getting warmer much faster than the average, with southern Africa witnessing roughly
twice the global warming rate over the last half century.
Improved farming practices have enabled Kenyan farmers to increase farm produce remarkably.

Africa is especially susceptible to the effects of global warming partly because it has difficulty feeding its
increasing population even without climate change.
The use of fertiliser and improved seeds can help Africa's small farmers impressively increase crop yields.

It has proved even more important to shift from traditional farming to setting up businesses that can bring
technology and investment to small farmers in Africa.
Everyone in the world will have to bear the consequences of climate change, especially Africans.
Improvement in farm output per worker in Africa falls far short of that in Asia.

In the long term, the potential for small farms in Africa to increase productivity is quite limited, especially
owing to the warming climate and a growing population.

passages.label: Passage One
passages.text:
Questions 46 to 50 are based on the following passage.
One of the great successes of the Republican Party in recent decades is the relentless propagation of a simple
formula for economic growth: tax cuts.
The formula doesn't work, but that has not affected its popularity. And while the cult of tax cuts has attracted
many critics, it lacks for obvious rivals.
Democratic politicians have tended to campaign on helping people left behind by economic growth. When
Democrats do talk about encouraging economic growth, they often sound like Republicans.
This is not just a political problem for Democrats; it is an economic problem for the United States. The
nation needs a better story about the drivers of economic growth. The painful lessons of recent decades point to a
promising candidate: higher wages.
Raising the wages of American workers ought to be the priority of economic policymakers. We'd all be
better off paying less attention to quarterly updates on the growth of the nation's gross domestic product (GDP)
and focusing instead on the growth of workers' paychecks.
Set aside, for the moment, the familiar argument for higher wages: fairmess. The argument here is that higher
wages can fuel the engine of economic growth.

Perhaps the most famous illustration of the benefits is the story of Henry Ford's decision in 1914 to pay S?
a day to workers on his Model T assembly lines. He did it to increase production—he was paying a premium to
maintain a reliable workforce. The unexpected benefit was that Ford's factory workers became Ford customers, too.
The same logic still holds: Consumption drives the American economy, and workers who are paid more can
spend more
Mainstream economists insist that it is impossible to order up a sustainable increase in wages because
compensation levels reflect the unerring judgment of market forces.
The conventional wisdom held that productivity growth was the only route to higher wages. Through that
lens, efforts to negotiate higher wages were counterproductive. Minimum-wage laws would raise unemployment
because there was only so much money in the wage pool, and if some people got more, others would get none.
It was in the context of this worldview that it became popular to argue that tax cuts would drive prosperity.
Rich people would invest, productivity would increase, wages would rise.
In the real world, things are more complicated. Wages are influenced by a tug of war between employers
and workers, and employers have been winning. One clear piece of evidence is the widening gap between
productivity growth and wage growth since roughly 1970.Productivity has more than doubled; wages have
lagged far behind.
A focus on wage growth would provide an antidote (矫正方法) to the attractive simplicity of the belief in
the magical power of tax cuts.

questions.stem: Why does the formula of tax cuts remain popular though ineffective?

options.A: Its critics' voice has not been heard throughout the country.
options.B: There seem to be no other options available to replace it.
options.C: The cult of tax cuts has been relentlessly propagated by all policymakers.
options.D: There appears to be a misunderstanding of the formula among the public.

questions.explanation: 。 定+稿搪赖千信意间fbrmula of tax cuts remain popular though ineffective司蒋客案线索比福室第二段。 位： 解;第二段提及，这个方案虽然不起作用，但这并没有影响它的受欢迎程度。对减税的推崇引来了许多批评人 析| ±,但它缺乏明显的竞争对手。由此可知，原文所说的“竞争对手”就是指其他解决方案，即没有其他可 |替代的方案，故答案为B。原文提及很多批评人士批评减税方案，但减税政策仍然受欢迎，这与他们的声 I 音是否在全国范围内被听到无关，故排除A项；C项是结合第一段设置的干扰项，但不停宣传减税不是减 税方案仍然受欢迎的原因，故排除；D项原文中没有相关信息，故排除。

questions.stem: What does the author think is a more effective measure for driving economic growth in the U.S.?

options.A: Aiding people left behind by economic growth.
options.B: Prioritizing the growth of the nation's GDP.
options.C: Increasing the compensation for labor.
options.D: Introducing even more extensive tax cuts.

questions.explanation: ,第四段第二句提及，关于经济增长动力，美国需要一个更好的故事。随后第三句说明，近几十年的惨痛教 析 训指向了一个大有可为的候选方案：提高工资。因此C项符合题意，compensation for labor “劳动报酬”指 的就是原文中所说的wages “工资”，increasing对应原文的higher。A项是民主党宣扬的做法，不是作者提 出的推动经济增长的措施，故排除；作者提及要少关注GDP季度增长，转而关注工资增长，故排除B项； 作者认为减税不起作用，D项与其观点相反，且原文中没有出现“大规模减税”的说法，故也排除。

questions.stem: What is the logic underlying the author's viewpoint?

options.A: The growth of workers' paychecks ultimately boosts the nation's economy.
options.B: Paying a premium to maintain a reliable workforce attracts more customers.
options.C: Consumption stimulates the desire for higher wages.
options.D: Familiar arguments for higher wages are outdated.

questions.explanation: ;第八段中作者指出，同样的逻辑仍然成立，即消费推动美国经济的发展，工资更高的工人消费也会更高。 析 也就是说，工资的增长会推动国家经济的发展，因此答案为A。第八段中的“逻辑”指的是第七段中亨 利•福特为工人支付高工资，本来是为了提高产量，却意外使工厂的工人也成了福特的客户，但作者举这 个例子是为了说明提高工人收入可以带动消费，侧重点不是吸引客户，故排除B项；C项原文缺少相关信 息，故排除；第六段作者提出，暂且抛开人们熟悉的提高工资的理由：公平，这里要说的理由是，提高工 资可以推动经济增长，由此可知，不是人们熟悉的提高工资的论点已经过时了，而是作者的论点（或者说 作者使用的逻辑）不在于此，故排除D项。

questions.stem: What is the basis for higher wages according to the conventional wisdom?

options.A: Fairness in distribution.
options.B: Increase in productivity.
options.C: The priority of economic policymakers.
options.D: The unerring judgment of market forces.

questions.explanation: $ 倒数第四段第一句指出，传统观点认为，生产率增长是提高工资的唯一途径，因此B项符合题意。根据第 析. 六段可知，A项是人们熟悉的提高工资的依据，故排除；C项原文未提及，故排除；根据第九段可知，薪 酬水平反映了对市场力量的准确判断，但对市场力量的准确判断并非提高工资的依据，故排除D项。 1 6 六级 2023.12 * - 套

questions.stem: What do we learn about things in the real world in America for the past 50 years or so?

options.A: People have failed to see a corresponding increase in wages and in productivity.
options.B: People have been disheartened by the widening gap between the haves and have-nots.
options.C: People have witnessed a tug of war between Republicans and Democrats over tax cuts.
options.D: People have seen the link disappearing between productivity and workers' well-being.

questions.explanation: 到什么? A）人们没有看到工资和生产率的相应增长。 B）人们对富人和穷人之间日益扩大的差距感到沮丧。 C）人们目睹了共和党和民主党在减税问题上的激烈争夺。 D）人们看到了生产率与工人福利之间的联系正在消失。 定？粗搪巅千彳言百、同real world、the past 50 years 位： or so可将答案线索定位至倒数第二段。 解，倒数第二段开头总结道，在现实世界中，情况更为复杂，工资受到雇主和工人之间激烈争夺的影响，而获 析 胜的一直是雇主。随后说明一个具体论据：大约自1970年以来，生产率增幅与工资增幅之间的差距不断扩 大，生产率增长了一倍多，而工资增长却远远落后。由此可知，A项符合题意。其他三项在倒数第二段中 均未体现，故均排除。 Passage Two ® •概览• 本文节选自The British Psychological Society （英国心理学会）网站于2022年2月2 2 日发表的一篇标题 为 “The majority of psychology journal editors are men and based in the US” 的文章。文章指出，《自然一 神经科学》杂志上的一项新研究发现，顶尖心理学和神经科学期刊的大多数编辑都是男性，这可能会加 剧该领域存在的性别不平等，并影响发表的研究的类型。文章继续指出，编委会的性别不平等问题并不 是缺乏女性学者导致的，而是现实存在一些不利于女性的障碍，积极打破这些障碍有助于实现期刊编辑 的多样性。 © ・全文翻译• 期刊编辑决定着发表什么，不发表什么，这影响着其他学者的职业生涯， 也影响着一个领域的发展方向° ［51］那么，人们会希望期刊尽其所能建立一个 多元化的编委会，反映各种声音、经历和身份。 不幸的是，《自然一神经科学》杂志上的一项新研究读起来让人沮丧。 ［52］该研究团队发现，顶尖心理学和神经科学期刊的大多数编辑都是男性,而 且都在美国：这种情况可能会加剧该领域存在的性别不平等，并影响发表的研 究的类型。 研究发现，在心理学期刊的编辑中，男性占60%。在各个级别的编辑中， 男性编辑都明

passages.label: Passage Two
passages.text:
Questions 51 to 55 are based on the following passage.
Journal editors decide what gets published and what doesn't, affecting the careers of other academics and

influencing the direction that a field takes. You'd hope, then, that journals would do everything they can to
establish a diverse editorial board, reflecting a variety of voices, experiences, and identities.
Unfortunately a new study in Nature Neuroscience makes for disheartening reading. The team finds that
the majority of editors in top psychology and neuroscience journals are male and based in the United States: a
situation that may be amplifying existing gender inequalities in the ficld and influencing the kind of research that
gets published.
Men were found to account for 60of the editors of psychology journals. There were significantly more
male than female editors at each level of seniority, and men made up the majority of editors in over three quarters
of the jourmals. Crucially, the proportion of female editors was significantly lower than the overall proportion of
women psychology researchers.
The differences were even starker in the neuroscience journals: 70of editors were male, and men held
the majority of editorial positions in 88of journals. In this case, the proportion of female editors was not
significantly lower than the proportion of female researchers working in neuroscience—a finding that reveals
enduring gender disparities in the field more broadly.
Based on their results, the team concludes that "the ideas, values and decision-making biases of men
are overrepresented in the editorial positions of the most recognized academic journals in psychology and
ncuroscicncc.
Gender inequality in science is often attributed to the fact that senior academics are more likely to be male,
because historically science was male-dominated: it's argued that as time goes on and more women rise to senior
roles, the field will become more equal. Yet this study showed that even the junior roles in psychology journals
tended to be held disproportionately by men, despite the fact that there are actually more female than male junior
psychology faculty
This implies that a lack of female academics is not the problem. Instead, there are structural reasons that
women are disadvantaged in science. Women receive lower salaries and face greater childcare demands, for
instance, which can result in fewer publications and grants—the kinds of things that journals look for when
deciding who to appoint. Rather than simply blaming the inequality of editorial boards on tradition, we should be
actively breaking down these existing barriers.
A lack of diversity among journal editors also likely contributes to psychology's WEIRD problem. If journal
editors are largely men from the United States, then they will probably place higher value on papers that are
relevant to Western, male populations, whether consciously or not.

questions.stem: What would we expect an editorial board of an academic journal to exhibit in view of its important responsibilities?

options.A: Insight.
options.B: Expertise.
options.C: Integrity.
options.D: Diversity.

questions.explanation: i 析； 根据题干信息词an editorial board of an academic journal可将答案线索定位于第一段。 第一段有两句话，第一句提到期刊编辑决定发表的内容，这影响一个领域的发展；第二句指出人们的期 望，即希望期刊尽其所能建立一个多元化的编委会，反映各种声音、经历和身份。原文中的diverse和选 项D的Diversity基本属于原词复现，由此可知选项D为正确答案。其他三个选项均在原文中找不到对应信 息，故均排除。

questions.stem: What do we learn from the findings of a new study in Nature Neuroscience?

options.A: The majority of top psychology and neuroscience journals reflect a variety of voices, experiences and identities.
options.B: The editorial boards of most psychology and neuroscience journals do influence the direction their field takes.
options.C: The editorial boards of the most important journals in psychology and neuroscience are male-dominated.
options.D: The majority of editors in top psychology and neuroscience journals have relevant backgrounds.

questions.explanation: 到什么？ A）大多数顶尖心理学和神经科学期刊反映了各种声音、经历和身份。 B）大多数心理学和神经科学期刊的编委会确实会影响其领域的发展方向。 C）心理学和神经科学领域最重要的期刊的编委会都以男性为主。 D）顶尖心理学和神经科学期刊的大多数编辑都有相关背景。 定*根据题干信息词the findings of a 位： new study in Nature 可将答案线索定位于第二段。 解《第二段第二句提到，该研究团队发现，顶尖心理学和神经科学期刊的大多数编辑都是男性。很显然, 析 选项C是对该句话的同义转述，其中male-dominated对应原文中的the majority of editors are male, most ［important对应原文中的t o p ,故选C。选项A与第一段提到的人们对期刊编委会的期望有关，不是研究发 ［现，故排除；选项B是根据第一段第一句所陈述事实所做的推理，也不是研究发现，故排除；选项D在 I 原文中没有相关信息，故排除。

questions.stem: What fact does the author highlight concerning the gender differences in editors of psychology journals?

options.A: There were quite a few female editors who also distinguished themselves as influential psychology researchers.
options.B: The number of female editors was simply disproportionate to that of women engaged in psychology research.
options.C: The proportion of female editors was increasingly lower at senior levels.
options.D: There were few female editors who could move up to senior positions.

questions.stem: What can we infer from the conclusion drawn by the team of the new study on the basis of their findings?

options.A: Women's views are underrepresented in the editorial boards of top psychology and neuroscience journals.
options.B: Male editors of top psychology and neuroscience journals tend to be biased against their female colleagues.
options.C: Male researchers have enough representation in the editorial boards to ensure their publications.
options.D: Female editors have to struggle to get women's research articles published in academic journals.

questions.stem: What does the author suggest we do instead of simply blaming the inequality of editorial boards on tradition?

options.A: Strike a balance between male and female editors.
options.B: Increase women's employment in senior positions.
options.C: Enlarge the body of female academics.
options.D: Implement overall structural reforms.

questions.explanation: $ 第七段最后一句提到，我们不应该简单地将编委会的不平等归咎于传统，而应该积极打破这些现有的障 碍。这些障碍是什么，需要考生往前寻找。该段开头提到女性编辑数量少不是因为缺乏女性学者，其实 女性在科学领域处于不利地位有结构性原因的，随后举例说明，比如女性的工资较低，面临更大的育儿需 求，从而导致其发表的论文和获得的经费较少，而论文少、经费低又导致女性学者达不到期刊任命编辑职 位的标准，这些就是女性面临的障碍。因此作者建议我们实施整体结构改革，故选项D正确。其他三个选 项不是作者的建议，故均排除。 Part IV @ Translation 。 ・难词译注• 老龄化社会an aging society 养老 the elderly care 受到普遍关注 receive widespread attention 模式model 多元化 diversified 政府引导 government guidance 社会参与 social participation 居家自助养老 home-based self-help elderly care 推行 promote 相结合combine 养老设施 elderly care facilities 升级 upgrade 包 •参考译文• In China, with the arrival of an aging society, the elderly care has received widespread attention. What people talk about the most is what kind of elderly care model should be adopted. Most people believe that the elderly care model needs to be diversified. Through government guidance and social participation, we can establish more and better elderly

sections.title: Part IV Translation
sections.directions:
For this part, you are allowed 30 minutes to translate a passage from Chinese into English. You
should write your answer on Answer Sheet 2.
sections.source: 在中国，随着老龄化社会的到来，养老受到普遍关注。人们谈论最多的是应当采取什么样的养老模式。多数人认为，养老模式需要多元化。可以通过政府引导和社会参与，建立更多更好的养老服务机构，改进社区服务中心，鼓励居家自助养老，还可以推行家庭养老与社会养老相结合的模式。随着政府和社会对养老服务事业投入的持续增加，养老设施将不断升级，服务质量逐步改进，老年人的生活将会更加方便舒适、健康快乐。
